title: Phase-resolved momentum and load Formation Flight Observatory
renderer: BirdFlowVisualization deterministic offscreen Metal HDR rasterizer

presentationGeometry.leaderAndFollower: two independently phased copies of the locked Deetjen OB F03 measured-derived complete dove surface sequence
presentationGeometry.loopClosure: 14 ms velocity-matched cubic Hermite presentation closure
presentationGeometry.flowSpatialFilter: mask-aware separable Gaussian radius 4 sigma 2 with hidden solid-gap fill from surrounding fluid samples; presentation-only
presentationGeometry.flowOpacity: joint normalized vorticity and absolute vertical-velocity signal with 0.025 minimum displayed signal opacity
presentationGeometry.flowVisibility: all 48 encoded phases render the filtered full-opacity presentation of adjacent real archived c20 fields; no temporal or spatial presentation transform enters solver results
presentationGeometry.wakeBridge: three age-colored ridges reconstructed from archived c20 vorticity and vertical velocity; cyan is young wake and violet is old wake
presentationGeometry.wakeBridgeLuminance: normalized leader q5 reflected-population exchange from every sample of the passed 4,820-step c18 focused trace
presentationGeometry.wakeIntersectionMarker: presentation-phase ring at the follower plane; a visual locator, not solver geometry or a measured vortex boundary

latticeBoltzmannLens.mode: presentation-only D3Q19 collision-streaming topology anchored to the positive-x archived c20 wake ridge
latticeBoltzmannLens.streamingCue: phase-locked outward packet on every moving link
latticeBoltzmannLens.focusedCue: gold positive-z packet luminance follows the complete passed leader-q5 source trace
latticeBoltzmannLens.evidenceBoundary: topology and q5 identity are exact; packet motion and lattice-cell frame are presentation cues, not local 19-population readback or force-bearing geometry

phaseResolvedLoads.source: the 100 leader/follower resultant force-vector samples stored in the locked c20 formation report
phaseResolvedLoads.interpolation: cyclic linear interpolation in leader wingbeat phase
phaseResolvedLoads.direction: the archived three-component resultant force vector for each flyer
phaseResolvedLoads.length: square-root display mapping of force magnitude divided by that flyer's cycle maximum
phaseResolvedLoads.glyph: GPU-batched, depth-tested, camera-facing dual-layer vector arrow rooted on each flyer
phaseResolvedLoads.evidenceBoundary: arrow direction is quantitative archived data; arrow length is a normalized presentation mapping and is not a scale drawing or a new force estimate

gpuPresentation.diagnosticDrawCallMode: one triangle-list batch for D3Q19 and both load vectors

presentationGeometry.cameraComposition: spherical figure-eight around the dual-dove wake bridge, lattice lens, and phase-resolved load vectors
presentationGeometry.evidenceBoundary: body is measured-processed; left wing is measured-outline-derived and gap-filled; right wing is a documented bilateral-reflection assumption; tail is derived from the measured processed surface
presentationGeometry.forceBearingGeometry: the archived c20 CFD and loads remain the Li-Nabawy prescribed-wing canonical; the dove surfaces and D3Q19 lens do not replace solver geometry
presentationGeometry.presentationOnlyParts: both complete dove surfaces, wingtip guides, field display transforms, wake ridges, intersection ring, D3Q19 lens, normalized load-glyph length mapping, HDR finishing, and camera path are presentation-only

output.loopMode: forward-only seamless loop; no reversed frames

predecessor.description: exact V10 D3Q19 collision-streaming binary retained before adding phase-resolved c20 flyer-load vectors

claimBoundary: The two load arrows preserve the direction of the archived c20 resultant force vectors, but their lengths use a per-flyer normalized presentation mapping and are not scale drawings. The D3Q19 lens reproduces the exact one-rest, six-axis, twelve-face-diagonal topology and the locked q5 positive-z identity, but its packet motion and cell frame are presentation cues rather than local population readback. The figure-eight camera, HDR finishing, and every field transform are presentation-only. The original c20 arrays and report remain the only CFD and load evidence. The dual Deetjen dove surfaces remain measured-derived presentation replays and do not replace prescribed-wing CFD, voxelization, loads, or power. Failed c16-to-c20 force convergence still blocks quantitative formation benefit and biological claims.
